title: Deal List
voiceover_text: The server seeds five sample deals on startup — spanning DOOH, audio, gaming, CTV, and retail inventory. Claude called the deals list tool and returned them all in IAB format with status codes, ad types, and CPM prices.

title: Create Deal
voiceover_text: Now we create a deal using natural language. Claude calls deals create, which validates through a Zod schema — name, ad types, floor price, and start date are required. An IAB-compliant deal ID is generated and the status is set to Pending, ready for a provider.

title: Send to DSP
voiceover_text: We send the deal to a DSP partner. Claude first calls providers list to discover available partners, finds the Mock Provider, then calls deals send. This provider abstraction layer means swapping in The Trade Desk or DV360 requires zero changes to the tool logic. The deal now has a platform deal ID and awaits buyer approval.
notes: Send prompt at ~35s through result at ~55s

title: Status Check
voiceover_text: Checking status reveals the dual-status model — seller is Active, buyer is Pending. This IAB pattern tracks both sides of every deal independently across all connected providers.

title: Confirm & Activate
voiceover_text: Deals confirm marks all buyer seats as Accepted and activates the deal. The buyer seat, provider, acceptance date, and go-live date are all recorded.

title: Pause & Resume
voiceover_text: Deals pause changes the status and notifies every connected provider automatically. The reason is captured in the audit trail — essential for compliance. One tool call propagates across all platforms.

title: Closing
voiceover_text: And resume restores the deal to Active across all providers. That's the complete lifecycle — nine MCP tools, IAB-compliant data models, and an extensible provider architecture. This open-source reference implementation is from the IAB Tech Lab Agentic Task Force. Thank you.
overlay: 9 MCP Tools • IAB Compliant • Open Source
